summary: Add 25% jitter to the 429 retry sleep; fall back to Backoff(attempt) when Retry-After header is absent so subsequent retries grow rather than re-sleeping the same 5s default.
reason: The previous retry loop slept for cliutil.RetryAfter(resp), which returns 5s when the Retry-After header is missing or unparseable. Across attempts the wait never grew, and a fleet of retrying clients would synchronize their retries (thundering herd). Two changes: detect resp.Header.Get('Retry-After') == '' and use cliutil.Backoff(attempt) in that case; wrap the chosen base wait with a 0-to-base/4 jitter so retries spread out. Jitter helper guards against base < 4ns so rand.Int63n never panics on zero.
validated_outcome: TestJitterBounds and TestJitterZeroBase pass (1000 samples per base, all in [0, base/4); zero-base returns 0 without panic). Full suite 148 tests / 21 packages green.
